segments.text: Jesus stepped into a boat, crossed over and came to his own town.
Some men brought to him a paralyzed man, lying on a mat. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the man,
Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At this, some of the teachers of the law said to themselves,
This fellow is blaspheming!
segments.speaker: Teachers of the Law

segments.text: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said,
Why do you entertain evil thoughts in your hearts?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which is easier: to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So he said to the paralyzed man,
Get up, take your mat and go home.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the man got up and went home.
When the crowd saw this, they were filled with awe; and they praised God, who had given such authority to man.
As Jesus went on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax collector's booth.
Follow me
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and Matthew got up and followed him.
While Jesus was having dinner at Matthew's house, many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with him and his disciples.
When the Pharisees saw this, they asked his disciples,
Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: On hearing this, Jesus said,
It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But go and learn what this means: 'I desire mercy, not sacrifice.' For I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then John's disciples came and asked him,
How is it that we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?
segments.speaker: John's Disciples

segments.text: Jesus answered,
How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while he is with them? The time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch will pull away from the garment, making the tear worse.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Neither do people pour new wine into old wineskins. If they do, the skins will burst; the wine will run out and the wineskins will be ruined. No, they pour new wine into new wineskins, and both are preserved.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman

segments.text: While he was saying this, a synagogue leader came and knelt before him and said,
My daughter has just died. But come and put your hand on her, and she will live.
segments.speaker: Synagogue Leader

segments.text: Jesus got up and went with him, and so did his disciples.
Just then a woman who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years came up behind him and touched the edge of his cloak.
She said to herself,
If I only touch his cloak, I will be healed.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: Jesus turned and saw her.
Take heart, daughter, your faith has healed you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And the woman was healed at that moment.
When Jesus entered the synagogue leader's house and saw the noisy crowd and people playing pipes,
Go away. The girl is not dead but asleep.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they laughed at him.
After the crowd had been put outside, he went in and took the girl by the hand, and she got up.
News of this spread through all that region.

verses.title: Jesus Heals the Blind and the Mute

segments.text: As Jesus went on from there, two blind men followed him, calling out,
Have mercy on us, Son of David!
segments.speaker: Two Blind Men

segments.text: When he had gone indoors, the blind men came to him, and he asked them,
Do you believe that I am able to do this?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yes, Lord
segments.speaker: Blind Men

segments.text: According to your faith let it be done to you
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and their sight was restored.
See that no one knows about this.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But they went out and spread the news about him all over that region.
While they were going out, a man who was demon-possessed and could not talk was brought to Jesus.
And when the demon was driven out, the man who had been mute spoke. The crowd was amazed and said,
Nothing like this has ever been seen in Israel.
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: But the Pharisees said,
It is by the prince of demons that he drives out demons.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: Jesus went through all the towns and villages, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom and healing every disease and sickness.
When he saw the crowds, he had compassion on them, because they were harassed and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
The harvest is plentiful but the workers are few.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Ask the Lord of the harvest, therefore, to send out workers into his harvest field.
segments.speaker: Jesus
